prevalence.source: ICMR/NFHS-5 (Indian Council of Medical Research/National Family Health Survey-5, 2019-2021 extrapolated)
prevalence.trend: increasing by 2-3% annually due to urbanization

riskFactors: Air pollution (PM2.5 exposure in urban areas)
Dietary factors (high intake of saturated fats, refined carbohydrates, and trans fats from ghee and fried foods)
vulnerablePopulations: People with family history of CAD
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (angina, myocardial infarction), supported by ECG, stress testing, echocardiography, and coronary angiography (per ACC/AHA guidelines adapted for India)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (~25% in urban areas, <10% in rural; via NCD programs)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~35% achieve optimal risk factor control (ICMR data)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Resource-stratified approach: Basic ECG in primary care, advanced imaging in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Medical management (antiplatelets, statins, beta-blockers); PCI/CABG in equipped facilities per CSI guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access in rural areas, high air pollution exacerbating cases in northern India (e.g., Delhi NCR), dietary patterns with excessive oil/ghee consumption, overburdened public hospitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Expanding Ayushman Bharat scheme for coverage, increasing cath labs in urban centers, national NCD program for awareness
